sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Organon & Co stock hits 52-week low at $5.76
feed.summary: Organon & Co (OGN) stock recently hit a 52-week low of $5.76, reflecting a significant 61.11% decline over the past year. Despite this downturn, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is in oversold territory based on RSI and is undervalued compared to its Fair Value. The company has recently presented data for its VTAMA cream, licensed MIUDELLA, and completed an independent review that found no misconduct.

feed.title: Sallie Mae and Nubank Shares Skyrocket, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Sallie Mae (NASDAQ:SLM) and Nubank (NYSE:NU) both saw significant jumps after reports from The Wall Street Journal indicated President Trump's willingness to end the military conflict with Iran. This news led to a broad market rally and a "risk-on" environment, benefiting financial firms like these due to anticipated stronger loan growth and improved credit quality in a more stable global economy. Nubank's shares, in particular, gained 3.7%, following a previous rally eight days prior spurred by similar geopolitical de-escalation news.

feed.title: Amazon ditches Amex for Mastercard
feed.summary: Amazon is ending its eight-year partnership with American Express for small business credit cards, choosing U.S. Bank and Mastercard instead. The new cards, set to launch on August 14, 2026, will offer various rewards for Amazon purchases, with no annual fees, and aim to provide small businesses with better cash flow management tools. This change specifically impacts small business credit cards and does not affect Amazon's consumer credit card offerings.

feed.title: Jury can't reach verdict in corruption trial of 2 ex-FirstEnergy executives in $60M bribery scandal
feed.summary: A jury in Ohio's corruption trial failed to reach a verdict for two former FirstEnergy Corp. executives, Chuck Jones and Michael Dowling, who were accused in a $60 million bribery scheme. The judge will consider a mistrial motion. Prosecutors alleged the executives bribed a future top utility regulator, Sam Randazzo, while the defense maintained the payment was an above-board legal settlement.

feed.title: W.R. Berkley Corp Stock: A Steady Player in Property and Casualty Insurance for North American Investors
feed.summary: W.R. Berkley Corp, a diversified insurer specializing in niche markets, offers stability for North American investors. The company's decentralized operating model and focus on complex commercial risks contribute to consistent profitability. With upcoming Q1 2026 earnings and a strong competitive position, Berkley offers a compelling investment opportunity despite cyclical industry risks.

feed.title: ThredUp, Genesco, VF Corp, Caesars Entertainment, and Sonos Shares Are Soaring, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of ThredUp, Genesco, VF Corp, Caesars Entertainment, and Sonos experienced a morning surge due to improved market sentiment. This optimism was driven by reports suggesting a potential de-escalation of the conflict in Iran and reassuring statements from Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell regarding inflation and interest rates. The positive news eased concerns over geopolitical tensions and monetary policy, leading to a broad-based rally in the stock market.

feed.title: Truist Financial Issues Pessimistic Forecast for Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:REGN) Stock Price
feed.summary: Truist Financial recently lowered its price target for Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:REGN) from $818 to $801, while maintaining a "buy" rating, indicating a modest 4.4% upside. Despite this somewhat pessimistic revision, Regeneron generally holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating among analysts, with a wide range of individual price targets. The company recently surpassed quarterly earnings and revenue estimates, but has also seen significant insider selling activity.

feed.title: Argus Raises Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) Price Target to $91.00
feed.summary: Argus has increased its price target for Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) to $91.00 from $80.00, reiterating a "buy" rating, which suggests a potential upside of approximately 9%. This aligns with a "Moderate Buy" consensus from other analysts, although Corteva recently reported Q4 EPS in line with expectations but missed revenue estimates, setting FY2026 guidance lower than current analyst expectations. The stock is currently trading near $83.47.

feed.title: Hershey presents snacking growth roadmap at investor day, confirms 2026 outlook
feed.summary: The Hershey Company presented its snacking growth roadmap at its investor day, reaffirming its previously announced 2026 outlook. The company detailed strategies for continued growth in the snacking sector. Further specifics on the strategic initiatives and financial projections were likely discussed during the event.

feed.title: Ex-FirstEnergy Executives’ Corruption Trial Ends With Hung Jury
feed.summary: The corruption trial of two former FirstEnergy Corp. executives, including ex-CEO Charles Jones and former VP Michael Dowling, concluded with a hung jury after more than eight days of deliberation. This outcome leaves the possibility for the Attorney General's office to retry the executives, as neither side secured a definitive win. The trial, held in an Ohio courtroom, lasted weeks and included testimony from US Senator Jon Husted.

feed.title: Weyerhaeuser's (WY) "Buy" Rating Reiterated at DA Davidson
feed.summary: DA Davidson has reiterated a "Buy" rating for Weyerhaeuser (NYSE:WY), setting a $31.00 price target that suggests a 26.5% upside. This decision comes as Weyerhaeuser reported an EPS of ($0.09), beating estimates, despite a 9.8% year-over-year revenue decline. Insider buying activity further supports investor confidence, with approximately 24,000 shares recently purchased by company insiders.

feed.title: SLB and Azule Energy Scale Enterprise Digital Operations to Strengthen Energy Delivery in Angola
feed.summary: SLB announced a three-year agreement with Azule Energy, a joint venture of bp and Eni, to expand the use of its Delfi digital platform across Azule's operations in Angola. This platform aims to enhance execution reliability, accelerate decision-making, and ensure reliable energy delivery for Azule, the largest independent energy producer in Angola. Early deployments of integrated workflows, including DrillPlan, have already demonstrated significant improvements by reducing planning cycles and increasing automation.

feed.title: Enphase Energy Storage Systems: Revolutionizing Home Energy Management for North American Investors
feed.summary: Enphase Energy's advanced IQ Battery storage solutions are transforming home energy management in North America, offering reliable, intelligent independence amidst rising renewable adoption and grid instability. These systems provide seamless integration, grid resilience, and long-term savings for homeowners, with features like high efficiency, Sunlight Backup, and AI-driven optimization. The market for these solutions is rapidly expanding due to federal incentives, state mandates, and grid unreliability, positioning Enphase as a key player for investors seeking exposure to the high-growth clean energy sector.

feed.title: Pfizer Inc Stock (PFE) Moved Up by 3.06% on Mar 31: Facts Behind the Movement
feed.summary: Pfizer Inc. (PFE) stock rose by 3.06% on March 31, driven by positive clinical trial results for its Lyme disease vaccine and oncology drugs and favorable analyst sentiment. The company announced successful Phase 3 trials for its Lyme vaccine candidate, prostate cancer drug TALZENNA, and breast cancer treatment tucatinib. Analysts have reiterated "Buy" ratings and increased price targets, with some market observers suggesting PFE is undervalued despite company-specific risks and ongoing financial pressures.

feed.title: Amazon Stock Is Priced For A Crisis — But Is It 2008 All Over Again?
feed.summary: Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) stock is currently trading at its lowest forward price-to-earnings ratio since 2008, suggesting investors are pricing in a potential crisis or recession. Despite this pessimistic valuation, the company's financial health and growth prospects, particularly in AWS and retail, appear robust, differentiating the current situation from the 2008 banking crisis. Analysts largely maintain a positive outlook, seeing significant upside as the market recalibrates these "crisis-style" fears.

feed.title: Weyerhaeuser (WY) Receives Maintained Buy Rating from DA Davidso
feed.summary: DA Davidson has reaffirmed its Buy rating for Weyerhaeuser (WY), setting a price target of $31.00 USD. This decision aligns with a generally positive outlook from analysts, with the majority recommending either a Buy or Hold position for the company. Weyerhaeuser, a leading forest products company structured as a REIT, benefits from tax advantages on timber harvest earnings and is projected to have an average target price of $31.27 from 11 analysts.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Group Launches Industry's First Tri-Branded Credit Cards
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group and Bank of America have introduced the Royal ONE Visa Signature and Royal ONE Plus Visa Signature credit cards, marking the industry's first tri-branded offerings. These cards allow members to earn and redeem rewards across Royal Caribbean, Celebrity Cruises, and Silversea brands, enhancing the company's loyalty ecosystem. Benefits include accelerated points on cruise and everyday spending, as well as travel perks like priority boarding and anniversary rewards.

feed.title: Affiliate selling 5,698 EOG shares (NYSE: EOG) disclosed in Form 144
feed.summary: EOG Resources (NYSE: EOG) has filed a Form 144 notice indicating an affiliate proposes to sell 5,698 shares of common stock, registered as compensation on March 31, 2026. The filing highlights sales made by Jeffrey R. Leitzell, including transactions on December 31, 2025, February 19, 2026, March 2, 2026, and March 3, 2026, generating total proceeds of over $945,000 for 7,774 shares sold across these dates. This notice allows brokers to execute resale transactions under applicable rules without immediate registration, promoting transparency in the market regarding large share dispositions.

feed.title: IT Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Gartner, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: The Rosen Law Firm reminds purchasers of Gartner, Inc. (NYSE: IT) common stock during the period of February 4, 2025, to February 2, 2026, about the upcoming May 18, 2026, deadline to seek lead plaintiff status in a securities fraud lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Gartner made false and misleading statements regarding its growth rates and ability to meet consulting revenue targets and maintain contract value growth, causing damages to investors. Investors who wish to join the class action are encouraged to contact the Rosen Law Firm.

feed.title: Skeena Gold to refinance B.C. project with US$750 million note offering
feed.summary: Skeena Gold & Silver Resources Ltd. announced a US$750 million senior secured note offering to refinance its Eskay Creek project in British Columbia. The proceeds will also fund a partial buyback of an existing gold stream, aiming to improve future operating margins, increase exposure to gold prices, and enhance project economics. The Eskay Creek project is fully permitted and under construction, expected to begin production in Q2 2027.

feed.title: Tractor Supply Co. to open store in Pulaski, its first in Brown County
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Co. is planning to open its first store in Brown County, located in Pulaski, by early next year. Real estate developer CMK Properties purchased property for the new nearly 22,000-square-foot store, with construction expected to begin in April and the store to open in December, followed by a grand opening in January. This will be the 37th Tractor Supply store in Wisconsin and the 10th built by CMK Properties for the "rural lifestyle retailer."

feed.title: Master the Outdoors: Ford and onX Bring Off-Road Adventure to Your Dashboard
feed.summary: Ford is partnering with onX Offroad to integrate their trail navigation technology directly into the dashboards of new Ford vehicles. This collaboration will allow Ford owners to access onX Offroad's comprehensive trail maps, navigation, and trail information without needing a separate device, enhancing the off-road adventure experience. The integration aims to simplify exploring the outdoors by bringing detailed mapping and critical trail data directly to the vehicle's infotainment system.

feed.title: Why is ONEOK (OKE) One of the Most Profitable NYSE Stocks to Invest In?
feed.summary: ONEOK (OKE) is highlighted as one of the most profitable NYSE stocks, receiving an upgrade from Wells Fargo to Overweight with a price target of $100 due to anticipated shifts in global energy markets and increased demand for U.S. energy. Truist also initiated coverage with a Hold rating and a $91 price target, recognizing ONEOK's extensive assets across various natural gas and liquid products. The article emphasizes ONEOK's robust operations in natural gas gathering, processing, liquids, and pipelines, making it a strong contender in the energy sector.

feed.title: KT names B2B veteran as new CEO, signals AI shift
feed.summary: KT Corp. has appointed Park Yoon-young as its new CEO, marking a strategic shift towards artificial intelligence and management stabilization. Park plans to focus on strengthening the company's fundamentals, expanding into AI-driven services for both consumer and enterprise segments, and advancing future technologies like 6G. The company also announced a significant organizational reshuffle and executive cut to enhance agility and promote performance-driven leaders.

feed.title: Unilever & McCormick Join Forces to Create Flavour Giant
feed.summary: Unilever and McCormick & Company have announced a merger to create a global flavor powerhouse, combining Unilever’s Foods business with McCormick. This transaction, valued at approximately $44.8 billion for Unilever Foods, will result in a company with leading brands like McCormick, Knorr, and Hellmann’s, and projected revenues of $20 billion based on fiscal year 2025 data. For Unilever, the separation will position it as a pureplay HPC company with increased focus on high-growth categories, while McCormick will retain its name, headquarters, and leadership, with an international headquarters in the Netherlands.
feed.source: Abasto, Food and Beverage Industry News
feed.source_domain: Abasto, Food and Beverage Industry News

feed.title: Is Simon Property Group, Inc. (SPG) The Most Profitable Real Estate Stock Right Now?
feed.summary: Simon Property Group, Inc. (SPG) is considered one of the five most profitable real estate stocks. Recent analyst updates include Scotiabank raising its price target to $192 and Barclays reiterating an Equalweight rating with a $193 target, following leadership changes. The company, founded in 1993, is a REIT specializing in retail properties like malls and outlets, with analysts noting its stable strategic direction and disciplined capital allocation.

feed.title: US FDA warns of liver injury cases tied to Amgen's rare disease drug
feed.summary: The U.S. FDA has issued a warning regarding liver injury cases associated with Amgen's rare disease drug, Tavneos, identifying 76 cases, including seven of vanishing bile duct syndrome and eight deaths. This safety warning follows a previous request from the FDA for Amgen to voluntarily withdraw the drug due to concerns about primary endpoint data, which Amgen declined. The European Medicines Agency also initiated a review of Tavneos over data integrity questions.

feed.title: Executive Carter (NYSE: PKG) gains stock awards; shares withheld for taxes
feed.summary: Charles J. Carter, EVP of Mill Operations at Packaging Corp of America (NYSE: PKG), received equity compensation including 8,282 shares from TSR performance units and 634 shares from accumulated dividends. The company withheld 3,509 shares at $212.25 each to cover tax obligations. Following these transactions, Carter directly holds 50,621 common shares and 6,550 shares indirectly through a 401(k) plan.

feed.title: RTX Secures $3.8B Contract Modification for F135 Engines
feed.summary: RTX's subsidiary, Pratt & Whitney, has secured a significant $3.8 billion contract adjustment for the production of its F135 engines, which power the F-35 Lightning II. This adjustment finalizes details for lot 18 and includes provisions for lot 19, bringing the total contract value for both lots to $6.6 billion. The article also provides a financial health analysis and market sentiment for RTX, highlighting both strengths and cautionary signs.

feed.title: Biogen Secures ‘Running Start’ in Kidney Disease With $5.6B Apellis Buy
feed.summary: Biogen has acquired Apellis Pharmaceuticals for $5.6 billion, a move that provides Biogen with two approved medicines and critical expertise in the nephrology space. This acquisition is expected to give Biogen a "running start" for the future launch of its own kidney disease asset, felzartamab, which is currently in multiple Phase 3 trials. Despite concerns from analysts about the premium paid and competitive dynamics, Biogen believes the deal will diversify its portfolio and accelerate the market entry of its new kidney disease drug.

feed.title: SAP SE Stock (SAP) Opened Up by 3.41% on Mar 31: What Signal Does It Send?
feed.summary: SAP SE's stock rose by 3.41% due to strategic advancements in AI and cloud initiatives, including the acquisition of Reltio to make enterprise data "AI-ready" and innovations in its Q1 2026 HANA Cloud release. This positive movement occurred despite recent analyst downgrades, suggesting the market is prioritizing SAP's proactive steps in enhancing its AI and cloud offerings. The company's share repurchase program, initiated in February 2026, may also be contributing to the stock's support.

feed.title: Sell Alert: William W. Sheehan II Cashes Out $1.06M In Ross Stores Stock
feed.summary: William W. Sheehan II, EVP at Ross Stores (NASDAQ: ROST), recently sold 4,883 shares of company stock for a total of $1,059,367. This insider transaction occurred on March 30, according to an SEC filing. Ross Stores shares were trading up 1.94% at $212.84 in the morning session following the news.

feed.title: Danaher Stock Is Down 20% in 2026. Here’s Why Analysts Still See a $239 Target
feed.summary: Danaher Corporation (DHR) stock has dropped nearly 20% in 2026, largely influenced by its $9.9 billion acquisition of Masimo, which caused investor concerns about the company's strategic direction. Despite this, analysts still project a target price of $239, implying a nearly 30% upside. The valuation model expects 9.9% annualized returns over the next 2.7 years, contingent on the company achieving anticipated revenue growth, margin improvement, and successful integration of the Masimo acquisition.

feed.title: Chubb will release Q1 results after market close on April 21
feed.summary: Chubb Limited (NYSE: CB) announced it will host its first-quarter earnings conference call on Wednesday, April 22, 2026, at 8:30 a.m. Eastern. The company plans to release its Q1 earnings and financial supplement after the market closes on Tuesday, April 21, 2026. Interested parties can access the live webcast or dial-in using provided numbers; a replay will also be available online.

feed.title: KKR and Taiyo Holdings Agree to Privatization to Accelerate Long-Term Growth
feed.summary: KKR is acquiring Taiyo Holdings through a tender offer at JPY 4,750 per share, representing a significant premium over recent market prices. Taiyo Holdings, a manufacturer of electronic materials and a medical/pharmaceutical business, seeks privatization to accelerate its long-term growth strategy by focusing on growth opportunities in AI, data centers, and expanding its contract manufacturing. KKR's deep sector experience and global network are expected to support Taiyo Holdings' strategic initiatives.

feed.title: Broadcom: The Bear Case Nobody Wants To Hear (NASDAQ:AVGO)
feed.summary: Despite strong AI-driven growth and VMware integration, Broadcom (AVGO) faces significant challenges, leading to a Sell rating. The company's revenue is heavily concentrated among a few hyperscalers, making it vulnerable to shifts in AI investment. While the aggressive VMware subscription transition boosts margins, it risks future customer attrition and income instability.

feed.title: Eastman Chemical trying to close in on key technical benchmark
feed.summary: This article states that Eastman Chemical (EMN) is approaching a key technical benchmark. No additional details are provided in the current content to elaborate on what this benchmark entails or the company's financial performance.

feed.title: These Virginia grocery stores are cheaper than Walmart, study finds
feed.summary: A new Consumer Reports study reveals that several Virginia grocery stores offer cheaper prices than Walmart. Specifically, Costco, BJ's Wholesale Club, Lidl, and Aldi were found to have lower average costs for common items. While Walmart was cheaper than many other nationwide chains like Target and Whole Foods, these findings suggest consumers in Virginia have even more economical options.

feed.title: AMAT Deepens its AI-Centric Product Expertise: Will it Deliver Growth?
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) is benefiting from high demand for its wafer equipment, driven by the increasing use of advanced chips in AI data centers. The company has formed partnerships with SK hynix and Micron Technology to advance next-generation memory and is seeing strong growth in its Logic and DRAM segments. Despite trading at a higher price-to-sales ratio than its industry average, AMAT's fiscal 2026 and 2027 earnings estimates show significant year-over-year growth, indicating a positive outlook.

feed.title: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) to Supply GPUs to AWS
feed.summary: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) has secured a deal with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to supply 1 million GPUs along with other chips through 2027, according to a Reuters report. The agreement, expected to commence this year, aligns with NVIDIA's ambitious sales outlook for its Rubin and Blackwell chip families. Beyond GPUs, the deal includes Nvidia’s new Groq chips and ConnectX and SpectrumX equipment for AWS data centers.

feed.title: Parnassus Mid Cap Fund Exited Pool Corp. (POOL) Due to Sluggish Demand in the Pool Market
feed.summary: Parnassus Mid Cap Fund exited its position in Pool Corporation (POOL) in the fourth quarter of 2025. The decision was attributed to the stock's high valuation compared to other distributors and sluggish end-market demand for new pools, despite the fund's overall outperformance of the Russell Midcap Index, driven by Information Technology and Utilities holdings. The fund remains optimistic about mid-cap stocks in 2026 but believes that some AI stocks offer greater potential for higher returns in a shorter timeframe.

feed.title: Monolithic Power Systems Stock: Power Management Leader Faces Volatility Amid AI Boom and Dividend H
feed.summary: Monolithic Power Systems (NASDAQ: MPWR) reported strong Q4 earnings, with revenue up 20.8% year-over-year to $751.16 million and EPS of $4.79, exceeding analyst expectations. The company also increased its quarterly dividend to $2.00 per share, signaling confidence in its financial health. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus with an average price target of $1,218.42, driven by MPWR's leadership in power management solutions for AI, cloud computing, and other high-growth sectors, despite facing stock volatility.

feed.title: Mizuho Boosts CF Industries (NYSE:CF) Price Target to $105.00
feed.summary: Mizuho increased its price target for CF Industries (NYSE:CF) to $105.00 from $100.00 but maintained an "underperform" rating, indicating a potential downside of 21.98%. Despite mixed analyst ratings, with an average target of $104.93 and some firms like UBS and BMO setting targets as high as $140.00, the company recently surpassed quarterly EPS estimates. However, insiders have sold approximately 82,408 shares worth about $9.97 million in the last three months.

feed.title: Chipotle Is Giving Away $2 Million in Free Food This Week, Here’s How to Get Yours
feed.summary: Chipotle is bringing back its popular "Burrito Vault" online game, offering over $2 million in prizes, including BOGO entrees, double protein, and free burritos for a year. The game runs from March 30 to April 1, with chances to win hourly. Additionally, Chipotle will offer a $0 delivery fee on April 2 to celebrate National Burrito Day.

feed.title: Lennar (NYSE:LEN) Given New $90.00 Price Target at Truist Financial
feed.summary: Truist Financial has lowered its price target for Lennar (NYSE:LEN) from $95.00 to $90.00, maintaining a "hold" rating, following the company's recent earnings miss. This adjustment suggests a potential upside of 4.27% from the stock's last close. Other analysts have also issued varied ratings, contributing to a consensus "Reduce" rating and an average target price of $101.71 for the home construction company.

feed.title: What You Need To Know Ahead of Ameriprise Financial's Earnings Release
feed.summary: Ameriprise Financial (AMP) is set to release its fiscal Q1 2026 results on April 23, with analysts forecasting a 9.7% growth in adjusted operating EPS to $10.42. The company has a history of exceeding earnings estimates and analysts are cautiously optimistic with a "Moderate Buy" rating and a potential upside of 29.2%. Despite its recent strong Q4 2025 performance, AMP's shares have underperformed the S&P 500 over the past year.

feed.title: Progressive Corp stock hits 52-week low at $197.83
feed.summary: Progressive Corp (PGR) stock has fallen to a 52-week low of $197.83, marking a 30.37% decline over the past year. Despite this, the stock shows potential value with a P/E ratio of 10.34 and a PEG ratio of 0.31, and InvestingPro analysis suggests it is undervalued. Analysts have had varied reactions to the company's recent performance, with some revising price targets downwards while others anticipate growth.

feed.title: Better Stock To Buy In 2026: Costco or Walmart
feed.summary: This article compares Costco (COST) and Walmart (WMT) to determine which is a better investment in 2026. While Costco boasts strong membership renewals and comparable sales growth, Walmart's diversified growth engine, driven by e-commerce and a rapidly expanding advertising segment, offers greater earnings expansion potential. Despite higher valuations for both, Walmart's strategic edge and margin expansion opportunities give it an advantage over Costco's membership-focused model with structurally thin margins.

feed.title: SNDX Analyst Rating Update: Mizuho Lowers Price Target | SNDX St
feed.summary: Mizuho maintained an "Outperform" rating on Syndax Pharmaceuticals (SNDX) but lowered its price target from $45.00 to $43.00, representing a 4.44% decrease. This update reflects ongoing analyst evaluation of the company's performance. The average target price from 12 analysts is $39.75, implying an upside of 68.97% from the current price, with a consensus "Outperform" recommendation.

feed.title: Packaging Corp (NYSE: PKG) CFO gets TSR stock awards, shares withheld for taxes
feed.summary: Packaging Corp of America's EVP & CFO, Kent A. Pflederer, reported equity compensation activity, acquiring 5,596 shares from TSR performance units and 428 shares from dividend equivalents after performance was certified at 200%. The company then withheld 2,670 shares at $212.25 each to cover tax obligations related to these vesting awards. Following these transactions, Pflederer directly holds 73,061 shares, with an additional 4,242 shares held indirectly through a 401(k) plan.
